title: Embedding Worker (Oban)

story.i_want_to: automatically generate vector embeddings for articles via background Oban jobs
story.so_that: semantic search stays current without blocking article creation

rationale: Embedding generation requires an external API call (OpenAI, Anthropic, or similar) that takes 200-2000ms. Running this synchronously during article creation would degrade the API response time and couple article writes to embedding provider availability. An Oban worker decouples these concerns: articles are created instantly, and embeddings are generated asynchronously. The circuit breaker pattern (snooze on consecutive failures) prevents hammering a down provider, while compile-time DI via Application.compile_env makes the embedding provider trivially mockable in tests.

acceptance_criteria.description: A `Loopctl.Workers.ArticleEmbeddingWorker` Oban worker exists in the `:embeddings` queue. It accepts `%{"article_id" => id, "tenant_id" => tenant_id}` as job args.

The worker is enqueued within the same Ecto.Multi transaction that creates or updates an article. Uses `Oban.insert/2` within the Multi to ensure the job is only enqueued if the article write succeeds.

The worker calls `@embedding_client.generate_embedding/1` where `@embedding_client` is resolved via `Application.compile_env(:loopctl, :embedding_client, Loopctl.Knowledge.EmbeddingClient)`. The input is `"{title}\n\n{body}"` truncated to 32,000 characters.
A `Loopctl.Knowledge.EmbeddingBehaviour` behaviour defines `@callback generate_embedding(text :: String.t()) :: {:ok, [float()]} | {:error, term()}`.
On success, the worker calls `Knowledge.update_embedding/3` to persist the vector. On failure, the worker returns `{:snooze, 30}` to retry after 30 seconds.
Max attempts is 4. Backoff uses exponential delay via snooze: attempt 1 retries at +30s, attempt 2 at +60s, attempt 3 at +120s. After 4 failures the job is discarded.
If the article has been deleted between job enqueue and execution, the worker returns `:ok` (no-op) without raising an error.
The Oban queue `:embeddings` is configured with concurrency of 5 (configurable). Added to the Oban queues config in config/config.exs.
The worker uses `unique: [keys: [:article_id], period: 60]` to deduplicate rapid successive updates to the same article within a 60-second window.
config/test.exs maps `:embedding_client` to a Mox mock: `config :loopctl, :embedding_client, Loopctl.MockEmbeddingClient`. The mock is defined in test/support/mocks.ex.

test_cases.name: worker generates and stores embedding on success
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Test Article', body: 'Some content...', embedding: nil}) as article
MockEmbeddingClient expects generate_embedding/1 and returns {:ok, List.duplicate(0.1, 1536)}
steps: Execute ArticleEmbeddingWorker.perform/1 with %{article_id: article.id, tenant_id: tenant.id}
expected_results: MockEmbeddingClient.generate_embedding/1 was called with 'Test Article\n\nSome content...'
Article embedding is now stored in the database

test_cases.name: worker snoozes on embedding generation failure
steps: Execute ArticleEmbeddingWorker.perform/1 with %{article_id: article.id, tenant_id: tenant.id}
expected_results: Worker returns {:snooze, snooze_seconds} where snooze_seconds is based on attempt number

test_cases.name: worker handles deleted article gracefully
steps: Execute ArticleEmbeddingWorker.perform/1 with %{article_id: non_existent_article_id, tenant_id: tenant.id}
expected_results: No embedding client call is made

test_cases.name: article creation enqueues embedding worker
steps: Call Knowledge.create_article(tenant.id, %{title: 'New Article', body: 'Content...', project_id: project.id})
expected_results: An Oban job exists in the :embeddings queue for the new article's ID
Job args include article_id and tenant_id

test_cases.name: article update enqueues embedding worker
expected_results: An Oban job exists in the :embeddings queue for the article's ID

test_cases.name: input text is truncated to 32K characters
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Title', body: String.duplicate('x', 40_000)}) as article
MockEmbeddingClient expects generate_embedding/1 and captures the input
expected_results: The text passed to generate_embedding/1 is at most 32,000 characters
Text starts with 'Title\n\n' followed by truncated body

test_cases.name: exponential backoff increases snooze duration per attempt
steps: Call ArticleEmbeddingWorker.snooze_seconds/1 with attempt numbers 1 through 4
expected_results: Snooze duration doubles with each attempt

technical_notes: Worker module: `use Oban.Worker, queue: :embeddings, max_attempts: 4, unique: [keys: [:article_id], period: 60]`.
Compile-time DI: `@embedding_client Application.compile_env(:loopctl, :embedding_client, Loopctl.Knowledge.EmbeddingClient)`. The default implementation wraps an HTTP call to the embedding provider.

config/test.exs: `config :loopctl, :embedding_client, Loopctl.MockEmbeddingClient`. Mock defined in test/support/mocks.ex: `Mox.defmock(Loopctl.MockEmbeddingClient, for: Loopctl.Knowledge.EmbeddingBehaviour)`.
Input construction: `"#{article.title}\n\n#{article.body}" |> String.slice(0, 32_000)` — truncation prevents exceeding embedding model token limits.

Enqueue pattern: within Ecto.Multi, use `Oban.insert(multi, :embedding_job, ArticleEmbeddingWorker.new(%{article_id: id, tenant_id: tenant_id}))` so the job is only inserted if the transaction commits.
Snooze backoff: `def snooze_seconds(attempt), do: 30 * :math.pow(2, attempt - 1) |> round()`. Returns 30, 60, 120, 240 for attempts 1-4.
The :embeddings queue config goes in config/config.exs: add `embeddings: 5` to the Oban queues list. Set to `embeddings: 0` in test.exs if Oban is in :manual mode for testing.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.

The default EmbeddingClient implementation must use Req for HTTP calls with retry: :transient, following the pattern in Loopctl.Webhooks.ReqDelivery. Example: Req.new(base_url: config[:openai_url], headers: [{"authorization", "Bearer " <> config[:api_key]}]) |> Req.post(json: %{input: text, model: config[:model]})
For Oban job enqueue verification in tests, use assert_enqueued(worker: Loopctl.Workers.ArticleEmbeddingWorker, args: %{article_id: article.id, tenant_id: tenant.id}) from Oban.Testing.

This worker uses snooze-based retry ({:snooze, 30} with doubling) rather than Oban's built-in backoff because snooze provides more predictable delay when dealing with rate-limited external embedding APIs. This is a deliberate choice matching the WebhookDeliveryWorker pattern.
